mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the consumer price index signal rising inflation expectations that compress real discount rates and drive nominal equity re-rating, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL increases month-over-month over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month span, deteriorating labor income prospects trigger risk-off equity reallocations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months during any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous month-over-month contraction of retail sales and industrial production signals a coordinated demand-supply collapse where inventory liquidation and falling revenues jointly erode corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month and INDPRO declines month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of months when both RSAFS and INDPRO decline month-over-month over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment, month-over-month acceleration in consumer price inflation causes the market to discount earlier-than-expected monetary tightening, raising real rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 55% of quarters when CPIAUCSL accelerates month-over-month while FEDFUNDS is below 1% during any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts signal accelerating residential investment and construction employment, which expands downstream demand for durable goods and lifts corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of weeks when HOUST increases month-over-month over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals improving manufacturing capacity utilization and operating leverage, which drives margin expansion forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of months when INDPRO rises month-over-month over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3%, month-over-month declines in retail sales signal that restrictive monetary policy is successfully choking off consumption demand, prompting negative earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment gains above 200 thousand signal a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending growth and reduces recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of weeks when PAYEMS increases by more than 200k month-over-month over any 2-year rolling window.
